title: ANZTOX data processing
text: article explains anztox dataset derived. dataset contains machine-curated species-level toxicity data, based ANZTOX SQL database originally served QLD government subsequently served SETAC website. species-level toxicity data screened aggregated following documented principles ANZECC ARMCANZ (2000) post-2000 ANZG guideline revision process (Warne et al. 2015, 2018, 2025). Exact reproduction original guideline datasets possible due undocumented expert judgement manual processing steps fully captured algorithmically; however, transparent fully reproducible approximation applied. brief, species, chemical, water type, toxicity data first filtered records pre-populated (original source SQL) concentrationused value available. Salt alternative chemical forms mapped parent compound CAS numbers grouping, data toxicologically equivalent forms (e.g. cadmium chloride, cadmium sulfate → cadmium) contribute single SSD. Data categorised chronic, subchronic, acute; chronic data available species, subchronic acute data discarded; subchronic data available, reclassified chronic. species acute data existed, acute LC/EC/IC50 values converted chronic negligible-effect equivalents dividing default acute--chronic ratio (ACR) 10, following Warne et al. (2025) Section 3.4.2.2. Toxicity values summarised using geometric means within endpoint categories, sensitive endpoint selected. minimum eligibility threshold SSD fitting ≥ 5 species ≥ 4 distinct major taxonomic groups. Two datasets processed combined single pipeline: - toxicityvalue2000 (17,755 records), originally sourced , Microsoft Access database distributed CD-ROM ANZECC & ARMCANZ Water Quality Guidelines. toxicityvalue2016 (2,794 records), data underpinning subsequent 2015 2018 updates water quality guidelines . datasets cleaned, harmonised, combined toxicityvalue_combined_clean, feeds single shared SSD eligibility workflow producing anztox_data.

title: ANZTOX data processing
text: processing implemented single consolidated script: script prepares ecotoxicology data ANZTOX database Species Sensitivity Distribution (SSD) analysis — statistical method used derive Guideline Values (DGVs) water quality. pipeline five main stages: 1. Helpers — Three normalisation functions defined ensure consistent matching keys: normalize_cas strips CAS numbers digits , normalize_mediatype maps variants “Freshwater” “Marine”, normalize_name lowercases strips punctuation chemical names. 2. Inputs — Two additional files read: CAS parent lookup table (csv mapping chemical variants/salts parent compound, see details ) master DGV table ANZG Water Quality Guideline website: https://www.waterquality.gov.au/sites/default/files/documents/toxicants-dgvs-mastertable-april2026.xlsx. 3. Database extraction — Connects local PostgreSQL database, reads toxicityvalue2000 toxicityvalue2016 tables, de-normalises joining lookup tables (species, chemical, mediatype, endpoint, effect, testtype, etc.) produce two clean flat tables. 4. SSD eligibility & nesting — two clean datasets combined, chemicals grouped parent CAS via lookup, filtered keep valid concentration records. test type priority rule applied (chronic preferred subchronic, subchronic acute, per species/chemical/endpoint group). Concentrations geometric-mean averaged per species endpoint, sensitive endpoint per species selected. chemical×mediatype combinations ≥5 species ≥4 major groups retained (minimum required SSD based Warne et al. (2025)). nested list-column ready SSD fitting. 5. DGV matching — SSD-eligible chemicals matched back published 2000 DGVs see many historical guidelines can reproduced database data. summary table categorises matches unmatched DGVs likely derivation method.

what: Why exact reproduction of the ANZECC & ARMCANZ 2000 guideline input data is not possible
title: ANZTOX data processing
text: Despite access rich relational database includes toxicity values collated support 2000 guideline derivation, several steps original ANZECC ARMCANZ (2000) workflow replicated exactly: Expert judgement undocumented exclusions original guideline derivation involved expert review studies methodological acceptability. Decisions sometimes made inspection (e.g. excluding anomalous studies, preferring one endpoint another) without fully codified machine-readable rules. Endpoint selection within species species, sensitive ecologically relevant endpoint selected. often required subjective judgement across life stage, exposure duration, endpoint comparability (e.g. EC10 vs NOEC). database retains candidate endpoints encode one ultimately chosen. algorithmically selected lowest geometric mean across available endpoints given species chemical. Temporal contextual decisions values included excluded based context (e.g. relevance Australian/New Zealand waters, water chemistry applicability), inferred purely database fields. Therefore, dataset regarded curated candidate pool, exact SSD input dataset used 2000 guidelines. data used derive water quality guidelines directly without manual data curation expert review.

title: ANZTOX data processing
text: 2016 dataset presents challenge reference attribution: unlike 2000 dataset, includes direct reference_id foreign keys reference table, 2016 records contain datasource record text fields without explicit reference linkage. best-effort reference matcher implemented: Primary extraction: Numeric IDs parsed datasource record fields using regex (\d+). Lookup: extracted IDs matched lu_reference$orgrefnumber retrieve bibliographic data (authors, year, title, journal, etc.). Preference order: datasource-derived matches preferred record-derived matches. Fallback: match found, formatted using reference_bib convention 2000 dataset. 2,794 records 2016 dataset: - 22 records (0.79%) valid reference matches - 2,772 records (99.21%) remain unmatched low coverage reflects database schema design: 2016 datasource record codes reliably map orgrefnumber values reference table. Remaining unmapped records retain NA reference_bib column.

title: Endpoint 2016 to 2000 lookup build
text: ANZTOX database contains two separate toxicity tables — toxicityvalue2000 toxicityvalue2016 — use different endpoint label vocabularies. 2000 dataset uses compact set standardised codes (e.g. MORT, GRO, REP, DVP) defined endpoint lookup table. 2016 dataset uses richer less standardised set labels, including free-text entries, abbreviations, misspellings, resolved via two foreign keys (endpointmeasurement_id endpointfrompaper_id) endpoint table. lookup table bridges gap: maps every distinct 2016 endpoint label nearest equivalent 2000 endpoint code, enabling two datasets combined single harmonised endpoint field consolidated toxicity pipeline (data_explore2000_2016.R). iteration specifically refines mapping previously unmapped poorly mapped labels, including: Fluorescence / chlorophyll variants (Fluoresence, Fluorescence, Chl-, Chl-fluorescence, ChlA content) Plant-size metrics (Leaf Area, Number new leaves) Life-history misspelling variants (Longevity, Fercundity, Fercunity, Young per female) Additional ecological functional labels (Cell volume, Cellular biovolume..., Algal cell viability, Ability attach host) reproducible builder : Run regenerate output files. script uses -session objects (raw_2016, lu_endpoint) present R environment (e.g. running main consolidation script); otherwise opens temporary database connection infogathering PostgreSQL instance closes exit via .exit(). what: How the lookup is used in the main pipeline
title: Endpoint 2016 to 2000 lookup build
text: data_explore2000_2016.R, lookup loaded startup validated uniqueness. Within toxicityvalue2016_clean build step, joined 2016 data endpoint_raw derived: endpoint_mapping_missing flag retained toxicityvalue2016_clean downstream analysis can identify 2016 rows using unmapped (raw) endpoint labels rather harmonised 2000 codes. prevent rows reaching SSD eligibility workflow, group correctly equivalent 2000 endpoint data unless mapping added. main script also uses endpoint_2016_to_2000_lookup_dedup, deduplicated version produced load time keeps one mapping per endpoint_2016_raw (preferring non-NA endpoint_2000_code, highest n_rows_2016). guards future lookup file inadvertently contains duplicate labels.
code: mutate(endpoint_raw = coalesce(endpoint_measured, endpoint_paper)) |> left_join(   endpoint_2016_to_2000_lookup_dedup |>     distinct(endpoint_2016_raw, endpoint_2000_code),   by = c("endpoint_raw" = "endpoint_2016_raw") ) |> mutate(   endpoint = coalesce(endpoint_2000_code, endpoint_raw),   endpoint_mapping_missing = is.na(endpoint_2000_code) )

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: ssddata data-R package providing Species Sensitivity Distribution (SSD) benchmark datasets evaluating SSD software (ssdtools, Burrlioz). packages chemical toxicity datasets CCME, AIMS, CSIRO, ANZG, EnviroTox, US EPA ECOTOX database.

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: R/ — Roxygen2 documentation files ; logic except utility functions (get_ssddata(), gm_mean(), ssd_data_sets(), envirotox_data_sets(), list_datasets()) data/ — Pre-built .rda files (one per dataset); loaded via LazyData: true data-raw/ — Source CSVs org + build scripts; part package build; maintainer-tests/testthat/ — testthat edition 3 tests vignettes/ — Quarto-based vignettes inst/REFERENCES.bib — Rdpack bibliography \insertRef{} citations roxygen docs

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: Individual datasets: {prefix}_{chemical} (e.g., ccme_boron, aims_ddt) Combined datasets: {prefix}_data (e.g., ccme_data, aims_data) Prefixes: aims, ccme, csiro, anzg, anon, wqbench, envirotox datasets stored tbl_df (named list *_data aggregates incompatible tables) Individual dataset .R doc files end NULL; aggregate *_data.R files end "dataset_name" Aggregated derived datasets (e.g. envirotox_acute, wqbench_data) end "dataset_name" (NULL) manually authored docs

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: Pipe: %>% (magrittr) data-raw/ scripts; native |> also used newer scripts; pipe R/ package code Imports: chk, dplyr, Rdpack, utils — keep imports minimal Input validation: Use chk package user-facing function arguments (e.g., chk::chk_string(), chk::chk_flag()) Side-effects: Use message() — print() cat() exported functions Docs: Roxygen2 markdown; use Rdpack::reprompt() \insertRef{key}{ssddata} literature citations

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: Add source CSV(s) data-raw/{prefix}/ Add build code data-raw/{prefix}/DATASET.R using usethis::use_data(..., overwrite = TRUE) Create R/{prefix}_{chemical}.R roxygen2 docs ending NULL Update relevant {prefix}_data aggregate .R doc file Run source("data-raw/source_all.R") devtools::document()

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: _pkgdown.yml auto-generated data-raw/build_pkgdown_yml.R — never edit hand. Section structure: 1. Individual SSD datasets — per-chemical datasets grouped source_prefixes subtitles (aims, anon, anzg, ccme, csiro) 2. Aggregated SSD datasets — {prefix}_data aggregates per source org 3. Aggregated derived SSD datasets — datasets listed derived_topics: currently anztox_data, envirotox_data, wqbench_data; envirotox_data gets subtitle “Species Sensitivity Data EnviroTox Database” 4. Fitted SSD results — ssd_fits 5. Package functions — remaining exported functions Key vectors update adding datasets: - source_prefixes — add new org prefixes heading labels - derived_topics — add new Aggregated derived dataset names - accounted_for — add topics silently excluded reference page (use @keywords internal .R doc file well)

title: ssddata — Copilot Instructions
text: "v2" (default) — 53 current individual non-aggregate datasets "v1" — fixed hardcoded list 20 ssddata v1 datasets prefix vector e.g. c("aims", "ccme") — filters v2 datasets prefix; valid prefixes: aims, anon, anzg, ccme, csiro "wqbench" , "envirotox_acute" , "envirotox_chronic" "anztox" — splits corresponding aggregated dataset per-chemical named list "alldata" — combines *_data sources splits chemical across sources; sources Medium column (aims, csiro, anzg) split Chemical × Medium match individual dataset naming conventions (e.g. aims_aluminium_marine, csiro_nickel_fresh, anzg_nitrate_hard_fresh) split (character vector, default NULL) — splits dataset named column(s), appending values dataset names; absent columns silently skipped summarize ("geomean" default "none") — collapses duplicate Species rows via geometric mean, reports ; emits message() cases cas_lookup (logical, default TRUE) — set = "alldata", aligns CAS numbers across sources splitting (currently -op hook) Private helpers: .split_aggregated(), .apply_group_split(), .apply_dedup(), .harmonise_columns() Column guarantee: every tibble returned ssd_data_sets() (regardless set) guaranteed Species first column Conc second column. Column names standardised data-raw/ build scripts, runtime. Standardised column names (enforced data-raw build scripts): anon_* exception: genuinely species-anonymous — sequential labels ("sp. ", "sp. B", …) assigned .harmonise_columns(). Note: wqbench concentration units mg/L vs µg/L sources — resolved current normalisation. Note: aggregated values ("wqbench", "envirotox_acute", "envirotox_chronic", "anztox", "alldata") must passed alone — mixing prefix strings (e.g. c("wqbench", "ccme")) supported error. ````

title: Species Sensitivity Distribution Data
text: ssddata R package Species Sensitivity Distribution (SSD) benchmark datasets can serve reference standard testing evaluation SSD methodologies. package includes range data sets sourced Canadian Council Ministers Environment (ccme_data), Australian Institute Marine Science (aims_data), Commonwealth Scientific Industrial Research Organisation (csiro_data), Australian New Zealand water quality guideline website (anzg_data, datasets published date later 2000), well anonymous datasets supplied various parties (anon). source dataset indicated using pre-fix data name (e.g. ccme, aims, etc), actual chemical name following (e.g. ccme_boron). package also includes wqbench_data, large data set US EPA ECOTOX database, cleaned standardized wqbench package; envirotox_data, dataset based envirotox database; anztox_data, based ANZTOX database historically hosted SETAC AU website. Please see relevant reference page information dataset. can browse source code github. Top report error, request features data additions please post github issues page.

title: ANZTOX Species Sensitivity Data — anztox_data
text: curated dataset species-level ecotoxicity data. data sourced ANZTOX database, originally served Qld government SETAC, now maintained locally. data cleaned algorithmically (machine) processed Species Sensitivity Distribution (SSD) analysis using workflow developed mimic closely possible principles decisions documented (ANZECC ARMCANZ 2000)  (Warne et al. 2015)  subsequent updates (Warne et al. 2018; Warne et al. 2025) . Data encompass two source tables available sql file extracted ANZTOX database: toxicityvalue2000 (17,755 records), originally sourced (Sunderam et al. 2000) , Microsoft Access database distributed CD-ROM ANZECC & ARMCANZ Water Quality Guidelines. toxicityvalue2016 (2,794 records), data underpinning subsequent 2015 2018 updates water quality guidelines (Warne et al. 2015; Warne et al. 2018) .

title: ANZTOX Species Sensitivity Data — anztox_data
code: anztox_data

title: ANZTOX Species Sensitivity Data — anztox_data
text: tibble one row per chemical x mediatype combination columns: casnumber_grouped Grouped CAS number (character). chemicalname_grouped Grouped chemical name (character). mediatype "Freshwater" "Marine" (character). data List-column nested tibbles containing species-level toxicity data.

title: ANZTOX Species Sensitivity Data — anztox_data
text: data cleaning SSD eligibility workflow fully documented accompanying vignette: vignette("ANZTOX-data-processing", package = "ssddata") brief: Harmonisation: 2000 2016 datasets differed structure endpoint vocabulary de-normalised, cleaned, combined single table. Chemical variants salts mapped parent compounds via curated CAS parent lookup. Endpoint labels 2016 mapped 2000 effect codes. Test type priority: Within species x chemical x endpoint group, chronic data preferred subchronic, subchronic acute. Acute--chronic conversion: acute data available species, acute LC/EC/IC50 values divided default ACR 10 (Warne et al. 2025, Section 3.4.2.2) obtain chronic negligible-effect equivalents SSD fitting. Aggregation: Concentration values species x endpoint geometric-mean averaged. sensitive endpoint per species retained. Eligibility threshold: chemical x media combinations >= 5 species >= 4 distinct major taxonomic groups retained. nested list-column ready SSD fitting related functions. columns follows: casnumber_grouped grouped chemical CAS number, mapped parent compound applicable (chr). chemicalname_grouped grouped chemical name, mapped parent compound name applicable (chr). mediatype Water type: "Freshwater" "Marine" (chr). data list-column containing tibble species-level rows chemical x media combination. row represents single species contains columns: scientificname, commonname_species, majorgroup, minorgroup, endpoint, endpoint_concentration (geometric mean concentration ug/L mg/L, depending chemical), testtype (Chronic Acute), source_datasets (comma-separated source: "2000", "2016", ), n_acute_converted (count acute records contributed geometric mean ACR conversion).

title: ANZTOX Species Sensitivity Data — anztox_data
text: ANZECC, ARMCANZ (2000). Australian New Zealand Guidelines Fresh Marine Water Quality. Australian New Zealand Environment Conservation Council Agriculture Resource Management Council Australia New Zealand, Canberra, Australia. Sunderam RIM, Warne MS, Chapman J, Rose R, Hawkins J, Pablo F (2000). “ANZECC & ARMCANZ Toxicant Water Quality Guideline Database.” Microsoft Access database distributed CD-ROM ANZECC & ARMCANZ Water Quality Guidelines. Warne MS, Batley GE, van Dam RA, Chapman JC, Fox DR, Hickey CW, Stauber JL (2015). “Revised Method Deriving Australian New Zealand Water Quality Guideline Values Toxicants.” Department Science, Information Technology Innovation, Brisbane, Queensland, Australia. Prepared Standing Council Environment Water (SCEW). Warne MS, Batley GE, van Dam RA, Chapman JC, Fox DR, Hickey CW, Stauber JL (2018). “Revised Method Deriving Australian New Zealand Water Quality Guideline Values Toxicants.” Australian New Zealand Governments, Canberra, ACT, Australia. Updated version associated Australian New Zealand Guidelines Fresh Marine Water Quality (ANZG 2018). Warne MS, Batley GE, van Dam RA, Chapman JC, Fox DR, Hickey CW, Stauber JL, Fisher R (2025). “Method Deriving Australian New Zealand Water Quality Guideline Values Protecting Aquatic Ecosystems Toxicants – Update 2018 Version.” Australian New Zealand Governments, Canberra, ACT, Australia. Prepared Australian New Zealand Guidelines Fresh Marine Water Quality.

title: Acute Species Sensitivity Data from the EnviroTox Database — envirotox_acute

title: Acute Species Sensitivity Data from the EnviroTox Database — envirotox_acute
text: data aggregated following code provided Yanagihara M, Hiki K, Iwasaki Y (2024). “distribution choose deriving species sensitivity distribution? Implications analysis acute chronic ecotoxicity data.” Ecotoxicology Environmental Safety, 278, 116379. doi:10.1016/j.ecoenv.2024.116379 .  three exceptions: dataset also includes chemicals (1) bimodality coefficient > 0.555, (2) six nine species, (3) two trophic groups. logical column Yanagihara24 indicates whether chemical meets criteria used Yanagihara et al. (2024): bimodality coefficient <= 0.555, least 10 species, least three trophic groups. logical column Iwasaki25 indicates whether chemical included analysis Iwasaki Y, Yanagihara M (2025). “Comparison model-averaging single-distribution approaches estimating species sensitivity distributions hazardous concentrations 5\% species.” Environmental Toxicology Chemistry, 44(3), 834–840. doi:10.1093/etojnl/vgae060 . : 50 species least three trophic groups, excluding certain metals. See envirotox_chemical corresponding CAS Registry Numbers, envirotox_data complete list dataset.

Yanagihara M, Hiki K, Iwasaki Y (2024). “distribution choose deriving species sensitivity distribution? Implications analysis acute chronic ecotoxicity data.” Ecotoxicology Environmental Safety, 278, 116379. doi:10.1016/j.ecoenv.2024.116379 . title: Species Sensitivity Data from the EnviroTox Database — envirotox_data
text: envirotox data contains SSD datasets EnviroTox database 2.0.0 (Connors et al. 2019). datasets provided assessing general patterns SSD data testing code. datasets used draw conclusions toxicity individual chemicals. data aggregated following code provided Yanagihara M, Hiki K, Iwasaki Y (2024). “distribution choose deriving species sensitivity distribution? Implications analysis acute chronic ecotoxicity data.” Ecotoxicology Environmental Safety, 278, 116379. doi:10.1016/j.ecoenv.2024.116379 .  three exceptions: datasets also include chemicals (1) bimodality coefficient > 0.555, (2) six nine species, (3) two trophic groups. logical column Yanagihara24 $acute $chronic indicates chemicals meet original criteria Yanagihara et al. (2024): bimodality coefficient <= 0.555, least 10 species, least three trophic groups. logical column Iwasaki25 $acute indicates chemicals included analysis Iwasaki Y, Yanagihara M (2025). “Comparison model-averaging single-distribution approaches estimating species sensitivity distributions hazardous concentrations 5\% species.” Environmental Toxicology Chemistry, 44(3), 834–840. doi:10.1093/etojnl/vgae060 . : 50 species least three trophic groups, excluding certain metals. full reproducible workflow generate three component datasets data-raw/envirotox/DATASET.R.

text: Retrieves specific SSD dataset, filtering groups species applies geometric mean case duplicate records.

title: Get SSD dataset — get_ssddata
code: get_ssddata(   dataset_name,   filter_val = NULL,   use_gmmean = TRUE,   spp_vec = c("Species", "Genus"),   conc = "Conc" )

title: Get SSD dataset — get_ssddata
text: dataset_name name (chr) desired dataset ssddata. filter_val character string, indicating filter applied (value) (colname) column applies , separated "_". Must form colname_value. use_gmmean Logical indicating geometric mean applied. spp_vec group_by columns use grouping data applying geometric mean. conc name concentration (x data) column.

title: List SSD Datasets — ssd_data_sets
text: Returns named list SSD datasets ssddata.

title: List SSD Datasets — ssd_data_sets
code: ssd_data_sets(   set = "v2",   split = NULL,   summarize = "geomean",   cas_lookup = TRUE )

title: List SSD Datasets — ssd_data_sets
text: set string character vector controlling datasets returned. Options: "v2" (default): current individual non-aggregate datasets "v1": fixed set 20 datasets ssddata v1 one prefix strings e.g. c("ccme", "anzg"): filters v2 prefix "wqbench": splits wqbench_data Chemical "envirotox_acute": splits envirotox_acute Chemical "envirotox_chronic": splits envirotox_chronic Chemical "anztox": splits anztox_data chemicalname_grouped x mediatype "alldata": aggregates *_data sources splits Chemical Note: aggregated values ("wqbench", "envirotox_acute", "envirotox_chronic", "anztox", "alldata") must passed alone - combined prefix strings (e.g. c("wqbench", "ccme") supported error). split character vector column names split datasets returning. Columns absent dataset silently skipped. Column values appended dataset names column present. Default NULL applies additional splitting. summarize string controlling duplicate Species rows within chemical handled. "geomean" (default) applies geometric mean emits message; "none" returns data -emits message listing duplicates. cas_lookup flag. TRUE (default) set = "alldata", uses data-raw/anztox/cas_parent_lookup.csv align chemical names/CAS numbers across datasets splitting (currently -op hook).

title: List SSD Datasets — ssd_data_sets
text: named list tibbles. Every tibble guaranteed Species first column Conc second column. Datasets species information (anon_*) receive sequential species labels ("sp. ", "sp. B", ...). Additional columns follow original order.

title: Species Sensitivity Data from US EPA ECOTOX Database — wqbench_data
text: data sourced : U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (2025). “ECOTOXicology Knowledgebase.” Accessed 2025, https://cfpub.epa.gov/ecotox/ (visited ). data cleaning steps closely follow wqbench processing procedures resulting data similar. brief, wqbench processing compiles data ECOTOX database firstly classifying tests either acute chronic; converting acute data chronic based published acute chronic ratios; finally aggregating data single row individual species. Aggregation achieved grouping data life stage; selecting sensitive endpoint; selecting preferred toxicity estimate(s) event one available; applying geometric mean one value preferred toxicity metric available sensitive endpoint. deviations wqbench processing steps applied generalise data set beyond focus British Columbia (BC), part align technical guidance jurisdictions (example, Australia New Zealand, ANZG). Deviations wqbench processing steps include: chemicals six species included regardless whether contain species present BC. excluded wqbench designed aim representative BC. chemicals representative species four taxonomic classes included. done align somewhat methods ANZG (https://www.waterquality.gov.au/anz-guidelines/guideline-values/derive/warne-method-derive-2025) requires guidelines derived taxa four "distinct taxa". Using class define "distinct taxa" approximates requirement, definition provided ANZG linked directly one taxonomic hierarchical level therefore applied automated way using taxonomic hierarchy.

title: ssd_data_sets() — Update Summary
text: ssd_data_sets() significantly extended support multiple data sources, flexible dataset grouping, configurable duplicate-species handling. changes R/get_ssddata.R. stub file R/ssd-data-sets.R now contains comment redirecting get_ssddata.R. 25 unit tests pass (tests/testthat/test-ssd-data-sets.R).

title: ssd_data_sets() — Update Summary
code: ssd_data_sets(   set        = "v2",   group      = NULL,   dedup      = "geomean",   cas_lookup = TRUE )

title: ssd_data_sets() — Update Summary
text: Controls collection datasets returned. Dataset counts (current): Dataset names split sources constructed make.names() ensure valid R names, e.g. envirotox_acute_1.2.4.Trichlorobenzene, anztox_Aluminium_Freshwater.

title: ssd_data_sets() — Update Summary
text: Controls datasets split returned one column values. Applies set selection. control duplicate-species handling (see dedup). Default: NULL (additional splitting) Accepts character vector column names, e.g. group = "Domain" group = c("Domain", "mediatype") Columns absent dataset silently skipped — error present, column value always appended dataset name, e.g. aims_aluminium_marine → aims_aluminium_marine_Temperate, aims_aluminium_marine_Tropical Example:
code: ssd_data_sets(set = "aims", group = "Domain") # aims_aluminium_marine_Temperate # aims_aluminium_marine_Mixed # aims_aluminium_marine_Tropical # aims_gallium_marine_Tropical # ...

title: ssd_data_sets() — Update Summary
text: Controls duplicate Species rows within chemical handled grouping. Separate group. Invalid values throw informative error:
code: `dedup` must be "geomean" or "none", not "bad".

title: ssd_data_sets() — Update Summary
text: Type: logical, default TRUE relevant set = "alldata" TRUE, uses data-raw/anztox/cas_parent_lookup.csv align chemical names/CAS numbers across datasets splitting Currently -op hook lookup file bundled installed package; full implementation deferred

title: ssd_data_sets() — Update Summary
text: anztox_data nested tibble (list-column data per chemicalname_grouped × mediatype). \"anztox\" split unnests automatically — element returned list inner tibble chemical/medium combination. wqbench_data uses chemical_name (Chemical) chemical identifier column — handled internally. envirotox_data named list wrapper, flat tibble. Use \"envirotox_acute\" / \"envirotox_chronic\" directly rather \"envirotox_data\" set value. old getdata() helper specify package = \"ssddata\" utils::data() call, causing data set 'x' found warnings called variable name. internal loading .split_aggregated() uses utils::data(list = name, package = \"ssddata\", envir = e) directly avoid .

what: Confirmed Column Alignment Across Dataset Sources
title: ssd_data_sets() — Update Summary
text: Column names verified directly loaded R objects (names()). inner tibble anztox_data checked via names(anztox_data$data[[1]]). Notes: - dedup operates Species column source. column names differ, .apply_dedup() must use appropriate column name per source: Species individual datasets envirotox; scientificname anztox inner tibbles; latin_name wqbench. - set = "anztox" splitting, returned tibbles inner tibbles — chemical/medium context encoded list name, column. - wqbench_data concentration units mg/L (sp_aggre_conc_mg.L), whereas sources use µg/L (Conc). aware unit differences combining across sources.

title: ssd_data_sets() — Update Summary
text: 25 tests covering: set = "v1" returns exactly 20 datasets set = "v2" returns 53 datasets, named, tibbles Conc Prefix filter returns matching datasets set = "anztox" returns 174 datasets named anztox_* set = "wqbench" returns datasets named wqbench_* set = "envirotox_acute" returns 729 datasets group = "Domain" splits aims/csiro datasets appends Domain names group silently skips columns absent dataset dedup = "geomean" emits message duplicates present dedup = "none" emits message listing duplicate species Invalid set value throws informative error Invalid dedup value throws informative error ccme_boron dataset structure value ranges (regression)

title: Instructions: Updating ssd_data_sets()
text: ssd_data_sets() defined R/get_ssddata.R. existing get_ssddata() function (file) handles retrieval + geomean deduplication single named dataset — new ssd_data_sets() behaviour need replicate extend logic across multiple datasets. gm_mean() (file) geometric mean helper reuse.

title: Instructions: Updating ssd_data_sets()
text: Returns individual (non-aggregate, non-envirotox) datasets named list tibbles arguments Excludes: ssd_fits, *_data, envirotox_* datasets Current code:
code: ssd_data_sets <- function() {   items <- utils::data(package = "ssddata")$results[, "Item"]   items <- items[!items %in% c("ssd_fits")]   items <- items[!grepl("_data$", items)]   items <- items[!grepl("^envirotox_", items)]   items <- sort(items)   datasets <- lapply(items, function(x) {     eval(parse(text = paste0("ssddata::", x)))   })   names(datasets) <- items   datasets }

title: Instructions: Updating ssd_data_sets()
text: Splits corresponding aggregated dataset named list per-chemical tibbles, mirroring format individual dataset list. Dataset-specific notes: wqbench: source wqbench_data (flat tibble). Split Chemical. Medium column — group includes "Medium" absent, warn ignore. envirotox_acute / envirotox_chronic: source envirotox_acute / envirotox_chronic (flat tibbles). Split Chemical. medium column. anztox: source anztox_data (tibble data nested chemicalname_grouped mediatype). Default grouping includes mediatype — .e. dataset names anztox_{chemicalname_grouped}_{mediatype} default. Dataset names split datasets constructed using make.names() ensure valid R names, e.g.:
code: make.names(paste0("envirotox_acute_", chemical_name)) # "envirotox_acute_1.2.4.Trichlorobenzene"

previous_headings: Key required changes > New argument: set
title: Instructions: Updating ssd_data_sets()
text: Aggregates across data sources using *_data aggregated equivalents (individual datasets). Sources: aims_data, anon_data, anzg_data, ccme_data, csiro_data, wqbench_data, envirotox_acute, envirotox_chronic, anztox_data. Note: envirotox_data named list wrapper (flat tibble) — use envirotox_acute envirotox_chronic directly instead. Default group = "datasource" appends source prefix dataset names. cas_lookup argument (see ) applies .

title: Instructions: Updating ssd_data_sets()
text: Controls datasets split returned — .e. columns define grouping keys used partition dataset sub-datasets. Applies set = "v2", prefix sets, aggregated sets. control duplicate-species handling (see dedup ). Default: NULL (additional splitting beyond set already implies) Accepts character vector column names, e.g. group = "Domain" group = c("Domain", "mediatype") Columns absent given dataset silently skipped — error, warning (datasets Domain, others ) column present, dataset split column column value always appended dataset name, regardless many unique values exist, e.g. aims_aluminium_marine_Invertebrate, ccme_boron_Invertebrate Note set = "anztox": mediatype splitting applied default part set logic (via group). Passing group = "mediatype" double-split — avoid.

title: Instructions: Updating ssd_data_sets()
text: Controls duplicate Species rows within chemical (grouping) handled. separate group, controls dataset splitting. Type: single string (chk::chk_string()) "geomean" "none" valid; invalid values throw informative error shared deduplication logic extracted private helper .apply_gm_mean(dat) (see Relationship get_ssddata() ) called ssd_data_sets() get_ssddata()

title: Instructions: Updating ssd_data_sets()
text: Type: logical, default TRUE relevant set = "alldata" TRUE, uses data-raw/anztox/cas_parent_lookup.csv align chemical names/CAS numbers across datasets splitting FALSE, CAS harmonisation applied

title: Instructions: Updating ssd_data_sets()
text: get_ssddata() currently handles single-dataset retrieval + geomean deduplication. new ssd_data_sets() replicate logic scale. Recommended approach: Extract core deduplication logic get_ssddata() private helper (e.g. .apply_gm_mean(dat, spp_vec, conc)) call functions. avoids duplication. get_ssddata() removed — public exported function may downstream dependents. becomes redundant, add deprecation warning pointing ssd_data_sets() keep least one release cycle (use .Deprecated() lifecycle::deprecate_warn()).

title: Instructions: Updating ssd_data_sets()
text: Different source datasets use different column names concept. Column names verified directly loaded R objects. Notes: - dedup must use correct Species column per source: Species individual datasets envirotox; scientificname anztox inner tibbles; latin_name wqbench. - wqbench_data concentration units mg/L (sp_aggre_conc_mg.L), whereas sources use µg/L (Conc). aware unit differences combining across sources. - set = "anztox" splitting, returned tibbles inner tibbles — chemical/medium context encoded list name, column.

title: Instructions: Updating ssd_data_sets()
text: Tests go tests/testthat/test-ssd_data_sets.R. Cover: set = "v1" returns exactly 20 hardcoded datasets set = "v2" returns named list tibbles, Conc column set = c("ccme", "anzg") returns datasets prefixes set = "wqbench" returns named list split Chemical set = "envirotox_acute" returns named list split Chemical set = "anztox" returns named list split chemicalname_grouped x mediatype set = "alldata" returns something (smoke test; detailed content TBD) dedup = "geomean" emits message deduplication applied dedup = "none" emits message listing duplicates group = "Domain" splits aims/csiro datasets appends Domain names cas_lookup = TRUE vs FALSE (set = "alldata") Invalid set value throws informative error Invalid dedup value throws informative error

title: ssddata 1.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Added 30 new ANZG individual chemical datasets: anzg_alpha_cypermethrin_fresh, anzg_aluminium_marine, anzg_ametryn_fresh, anzg_ammonia_fresh, anzg_bisphenol_a_fresh, anzg_bisphenol_a_marine, anzg_boron_fresh, anzg_chlorine_marine, anzg_chromium_III_fresh, anzg_copper_marine, anzg_dioxins_fresh, anzg_diuron_fresh, anzg_diuron_marine, anzg_fipronil_fresh, anzg_fluoride_fresh, anzg_glyphosate_fresh, anzg_iron_fresh, anzg_iron_marine, anzg_mancozeb_fresh, anzg_manganese_marine, anzg_mcpa_fresh, anzg_metolachlor_fresh, anzg_metsulfuron_methyl_fresh, anzg_nickel_marine, anzg_nitrate_hard_fresh, anzg_nitrate_moderate_fresh, anzg_nitrate_soft_fresh, anzg_paraquat_fresh, anzg_perfluorooctane_sulfonate_pfos_fresh, anzg_picloram_fresh, anzg_simazine_fresh, anzg_simazine_marine, anzg_sulfometuron_methyl_fresh, anzg_zinc_marine. Added anzg_data aggregate dataset.

title: ssddata 1.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Added envirotox_data (list), envirotox_acute, envirotox_chronic, envirotox_chemical datasets EnviroTox database.

title: ssddata 1.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Added anztox_data aggregated dataset derived ANZTox database, accompanying Quarto vignette documenting data processing steps.

title: ssddata 1.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Added wqbench_data aggregated dataset Australian water quality benchmarking work.

title: ssddata 1.0.0.9000 (development)
text: ssd_data_sets() gains set argument supporting "v1", "v2" (default), prefix vectors (e.g. c("aims", "ccme")), "wqbench", "envirotox_acute", "envirotox_chronic", "anztox", "alldata" flexibly retrieve per-chemical tibbles data source. ssd_data_sets() gains split argument split datasets arbitrary columns. ssd_data_sets() gains summarize argument ("geomean" "none") controlling duplicate species handling. ssd_data_sets() gains cas_lookup argument CAS number alignment across sources. Column names standardised across datasets returned ssd_data_sets(): Species (first) Conc (second) guaranteed. Added list_datasets() deprecated wrapper backwards compatibility EnviroTox workflows.

title: ssddata 1.0.0.9000 (development)
text: Updated ssd_fits dataset align distribution naming ssdtools distinguish shinyssdtools results. Auto-generated _pkgdown.yml via data-raw/build_pkgdown_yml.R structured reference sections. Added Ayla Pearson package author. Documentation improvements throughout, including Rdpack bibliography references new datasets. Naming convention standardised (e.g. freshwater → fresh, marine suffix).

title: ssddata 1.0.0 (2021-09-13)
text: CRAN release: 2021-11-05 Release CRAN. Added ssd_fits dataset fitted SSD results. CRAN packaging fixes: license, URLs, return value descriptions.
